title: Egyptian Fermented Dumplings
description: A traditional Egyptian dish made with fermented ingredients and dense dumplings. Serve with your favorite salad or soup.

ingredients.name: Fermented Egyptian flour
ingredients.notes: Make sure to use a fermented flour for the authentic taste

ingredients.name: Dense Egyptian flour
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality dense flour for the dumpling dough

ingredients.name: Water
ingredients.notes: Use lukewarm water for the dough

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use a pinch of salt for flavor

ingredients.name: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: Use a mild olive oil for frying the dumplings

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Chop the onion finely for the dumpling filling

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince the garlic for the dumpling filling

ingredients.name: Cumin
ingredients.notes: Use a pinch of cumin for flavor

instructions: Combine fermented and dense Egyptian flour in a large mixing bowl.
Gradually add water and salt to the flour mixture. Mix until a smooth dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes elastic.
Divide the dough into small balls, about the size of a golf ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle.
Place a tablespoon of onion and garlic mixture in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough over the filling and press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Fry the dumplings until they are golden brown, about 2-3 minutes on each side.
Serve the dumplings hot with your favorite salad or soup.
Note: Fermented dumplings are best served fresh, but they can be refrigerated or frozen for later use.
